title: 10 best herbal teas for ingrown toenail
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ingrown toenail are natural remedies made from various herbs that can help alleviate the pain, inflammation, and infection associated with ingrown toenails. These herbal teas contain anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties that can help reduce swelling, fight off infection, and soothe the affected area. Examples of herbal teas that can help with ingrown toenails include chamomile, calendula, Echinacea, and goldenseal. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can experience relief from the discomfort and pain caused by ingrown toenails, allowing them to carry out their daily activities with ease. Additionally, soaking the affected foot in a warm bath with these herbal teas can further help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise hyssop tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with ingrown toenails. The tea can also help to fight off any potential infection that may occur when the toenail grows into the skin. Drinking anise hyssop tea or soaking the affected foot in it can help to alleviate the discomfort and promote healing of the ingrown toenail. Additionally, anise hyssop tea contains antioxidants that can help to boost the immune system and promote overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Anise hyssop tea's anti-inflammatory property, derived from compounds like rosmarinic acid, helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with ingrown toenails.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of anise hyssop tea, owing to its volatile oils and flavonoids, aids in preventing infection in ingrown toenails.
Analgesic: Anise hyssop tea's analgesic effect, attributed to its essential oils and phenolic acids, helps alleviate pain caused by ingrown toenails.
Antifungal: The antifungal property of anise hyssop tea, due to its monoterpenoids and flavonoids, assists in inhibiting fungal growth in ingrown toenails.
Circulation-boosting: Anise hyssop tea's ability to improve circulation, owing to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content, aids in promoting healing in ingrown toenails.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried anise hyssop herb.
Heat the mixture on high until it reaches boiling point, then lower the heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
After simmering, turn off the heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a cotton ball in the cooled anise hyssop tea and apply it to the affected ingrown toenail for 15 minutes, 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with ingrown toenail because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling and fight infection. The tea can also help to soften the skin around the ingrown toenail, making it easier to remove the trapped nail edge. Additionally, pot marigold tea has been shown to improve blood flow and promote healing, which can speed up the recovery process. Drinking the tea or using it as a soak can provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with ingrown toenails.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation around the ingrown toenail, providing pain relief and promoting healing. This property is important because inflammation can exacerbate the discomfort and slow down the recovery process. Active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection in the affected area, which is crucial for avoiding complications and ensuring proper healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tea promotes wound healing, which is essential for addressing the ingrown toenail. This property helps the body repair the damaged tissue and regenerate healthy skin. Active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, saponins, and tannins.

Analgesic: Pot marigold tea has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain associated with ingrown toenails. This property is important because pain management is crucial for maintaining comfort and allowing for proper care of the affected area. Active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, triterpenes, and volatile oils.

Anti-fungal: Pot marigold tea has anti-fungal properties that help prevent and treat fungal infections, which can be a common complication of ingrown toenails. This property is important because fungal infections can further complicate the healing process and cause additional discomfort. Active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, volatile oils, and alkaloids.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried pot marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the flowers, leaving only the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the prepared pot marigold tea twice daily for relief of ingrown toenail symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce pain and swelling associated with an ingrown toenail. The tea also has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help prevent infection in the affected area. Drinking lemongrass tea can also help boost the immune system, allowing the body to fight off any potential infection. Furthermore, soaking the affected toe in lemongrass tea can help soften the skin and toenail, making it easier to treat the ingrown toenail.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness around the ingrown toenail, providing relief from pain and inflammation. This property is important for ingrown toenail as it alleviates symptoms and facilitates healing. The active constituents responsible for this property are citral and geraniol.

Antimicrobial: Lemongrass tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection in the affected area, keeping the ingrown toenail clean and promoting faster healing. This property is crucial for ingrown toenail as it minimizes the risk of infection and potential complications. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are citral and citronellol.

Analgesic: Lemongrass tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by the ingrown toenail. This property is important for ingrown toenail as it improves comfort and allows for easier daily activities. The active constituents responsible for this property are citral and geraniol.

Antifungal: Lemongrass tea's antifungal properties help prevent fungal infections in the affected area, keeping the ingrown toenail healthy and promoting faster healing. This property is crucial for ingrown toenail as it minimizes the risk of fungal infections and potential complications. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are citral and citronellol.

Circulation-enhancing: Lemongrass tea's circulation-enhancing properties help improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. This property is important for ingrown toenail as it accelerates the healing process and alleviates symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are citral and geraniol.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of fresh lemongrass leaves.
Boil the mixture for 10 minutes over medium heat.
Remove the pot from heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove lemongrass leaves.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the eastern purple coneflower plant. The active compounds in the tea, such as echinacea, help to reduce inflammation and fight off infection. This makes it an effective natural remedy for ingrown toenails, which can become red, swollen, and painful due to infection. Drinking the tea or soaking the affected toe in the tea can help to alleviate the symptoms and promote healing. Additionally, the tea can help to boost the immune system, which can help the body to fight off the infection and prevent it from spreading.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial property of eastern purple coneflower tea helps prevent infection in ingrown toenails, as the active constituents, such as cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid, have antimicrobial effects.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of the tea reduces inflammation and swelling in ingrown toenails, with active constituents like cichoric acid and isoquercitrin contributing to this effect.

Analgesic property: The analgesic property of eastern purple coneflower tea helps alleviate pain in ingrown toenails, as active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids have pain-relieving properties.

Antifungal property: The antifungal property of the tea helps prevent and treat fungal infections in ingrown toenails, as active constituents like cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid have antifungal effects.

Wound healing property: The wound healing property of eastern purple coneflower tea promotes tissue regeneration and faster healing of ingrown toenails, with active constituents like chlorogenic acid and quercetin contributing to this effect.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried eastern purple coneflower flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove flower particles, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, twice daily, until the ingrown toenail improves.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce swelling, pain, and risk of infection associated with an ingrown toenail. The tea can be used as a warm soak for the affected foot, helping to soften the skin and make it easier to remove the ingrown portion of the nail. Additionally, fennel tea may help promote overall foot health by improving circulation and reducing inflammation in the body. However, it is important to note that while fennel tea can provide relief and help with the healing process, it is not a substitute for medical treatment and should not be used as a sole treatment for an ingrown toenail.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness around the ingrown toenail, making it easier to treat. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of fennel tea helps prevent infection in the ingrown toenail area, promoting faster healing. This property is attributed to the tea's high content of phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Analgesic: Fennel tea's analgesic property helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with ingrown toenails. This property is due to the presence of anethole, a compound that inhibits the production of pain-inducing substances in the body.

Antifungal: The antifungal property of fennel tea helps prevent fungal infections in the ingrown toenail area, ensuring proper healing. This property is attributed to the tea's volatile oils and flavonoids, which inhibit fungal growth.

Diuretic: Fennel tea's diuretic property helps reduce fluid retention, which can alleviate pressure on the ingrown toenail. This property is due to the presence of anethole, which stimulates the production of urine.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 teaspoons of fennel seeds.
Boil 1 cup of water for 3 minutes.
Steep crushed seeds in boiled water for 5 minutes.
Strain the fennel tea into a cup.
Drink the tea twice a day for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with ingrown toenail because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce swelling and fight infection. The tea's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin, help to alleviate pain and promote healing. Drinking licorice tea can also boost the immune system, which can help the body to fight off any potential infection caused by an ingrown toenail. Additionally, the tea's anti-fungal properties can help to prevent the growth of fungus in and around the toenail. By drinking licorice tea, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and pain associated with ingrown toenails, while also promoting the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness around the ingrown toenail, easing pain and promoting healing. This property is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tea's antimicrobial property helps prevent infection in the ingrown toenail, reducing the risk of complications. The active constituents responsible for this property are isoflavonoids and coumarins.

Analgesic: Licorice tea's analgesic property helps alleviate pain and discomfort caused by the ingrown toenail. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and liquiritigenin.

Antioxidant: Licorice tea's antioxidant property helps protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall foot health and healing. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and isoflavonoids.

Circulation-boosting: Licorice tea's circulation-boosting property helps improve blood flow to the feet, promoting healing and reducing the risk of ingrown toenails. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and chalcones.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove licorice root pieces.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a cotton ball in the tea and apply it to the affected area for 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that reduce swelling, pain, and redness. The tannins in witch hazel tea tighten the skin, drawing it away from the nail and allowing the nail to grow properly. Additionally, the antibacterial and antifungal properties of witch hazel tea prevent infection and promote healing. Drinking witch hazel tea or using it as a soak for the affected foot can provide relief and help treat ingrown toenails.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea reduces inflammation and swelling, providing relief from ingrown toenail-induced pain and irritation. This property is attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.

Astringent: Witch hazel tea tightens and constricts skin tissues, helping to alleviate redness, pain, and fluid buildup around the ingrown toenail. Tannins are the primary active constituents responsible for this astringent effect.

Antimicrobial: Witch hazel tea inhibits the growth of microorganisms, reducing the risk of infection associated with ingrown toenails. The antimicrobial properties are primarily due to the presence of gallic acid and other phenolic compounds.
Analgesic: Witch hazel tea has a soothing effect on the skin, providing relief from pain and discomfort caused by ingrown toenails. This is mainly due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.

Improving skin health: Witch hazel tea promotes skin health by increasing skin moisture and elasticity, which can help prevent the formation of ingrown toenails. This property is attributed to the presence of polyphenols, flavonoids, and saponins.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of fresh witch hazel leaves and 2 cups of water.
Boil the mixture on high heat for 10 minutes, then reduce heat and simmer for an additional 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove leaves, then let the tea cool to a drinkable temperature.
Combine 1 cup of the cooled tea with 1/2 cup of water in a separate container.
Soak a cotton ball in the tea mixture and apply to the affected area for 15-20 minutes, 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and fight infection, making it easier to treat an ingrown toenail. The tea can be used as a warm compress to soothe the affected area and promote healing. Additionally, goldenseal may help to relieve pain and itching associated with ingrown toenails. Drinking goldenseal tea can also boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off any infection that may be present. However, it is important to note that while goldenseal tea can provide relief, it is not a substitute for medical treatment. If you have an ingrown toenail, it is always best to consult with a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Goldenseal tea helps reduce inflammation around the ingrown toenail, providing pain relief and promoting healing. This property is due to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine.

Antimicrobial: Goldenseal tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection in the affected area, reducing the risk of complications. The isoquinoline alkaloids, particularly berberine, contribute to this effect.

Astringent: Goldenseal tea's astringent properties help to tighten and contract the skin tissue around the ingrown toenail, reducing irritation and promoting healthy growth. The tannins present in goldenseal are responsible for this astringent effect.
Analgesic: Goldenseal tea has natural pain-relieving properties that help alleviate pain caused by ingrown toenails. Berberine and other alkaloids contribute to the tea's analgesic effects.

Circulation-enhancing: Goldenseal tea may help improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. The increased circulation is attributed to the tea's overall anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: In a stovetop kettle, boil 2 cups of water for 5-7 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried goldenseal root to a tea infuser.
Place the tea infuser in a cup and pour the boiling water over it.
Steep the goldenseal root for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and discard the goldenseal root.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties can help reduce the pain, swelling, and redness associated with an ingrown toenail. Additionally, lavender oil, which is found in lavender tea, has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that can help prevent infection in the affected area. Drinking lavender tea may also help reduce inflammation in the body and promote overall health, which can aid in the healing process. However, it is important to note that while lavender tea can provide relief and promote healing, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for an ingrown toenail.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling around the ingrown toenail, providing relief from pain and discomfort. This property is important because inflammation can worsen the condition and make it harder to treat. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and flavonoids.

Analgesic: English lavender tea has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort and pain caused by an ingrown toenail. This property is essential for managing the pain associated with the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, lavandulol, and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: English lavender tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote wound healing. This property is crucial for preventing the ingrown toenail from becoming infected, which can worsen the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and flavonoids.

Antifungal: English lavender tea has antifungal properties that help prevent fungal infections in the toenail. This property is important because fungal infections can complicate the treatment of ingrown toenails. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and flavonoids.

Circulation-promoting: English lavender tea has circulation-promoting properties that help improve blood flow to the affected area. This property is important because improved blood flow can help promote healing and prevent infection. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the lavender flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before soaking your ingrown toenail for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with ingrown toenail because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce swelling and fight infection. The tea can be used as a warm compress to soothe the affected area, and its natural oils can help to soften the skin around the ingrown toenail, making it easier to treat. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with an ingrown toenail. Drinking lemon balm tea may also boost the immune system, helping the body to heal faster.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection in ingrown toenails, thanks to its active constituents like citronellal, geraniol, and linalool.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of lemon balm tea can help reduce swelling and pain around the ingrown toenail, due to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
Analgesic: Lemon balm tea acts as a natural pain reliever, helping to alleviate the discomfort caused by an ingrown toenail, because of its eugenol and tannins.
Antifungal: The antifungal properties of lemon balm tea can help prevent fungal infections in ingrown toenails, as a result of its citronellal, geraniol, and linalool.
Antipruritic: Lemon balm tea's antipruritic properties can help soothe itching and irritation associated with ingrown toenails, due to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to be extracted.
Strain the tea into a cup, discard the leaves, and drink it while it's still warm.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ingrown toenail with herbal teas is to soak the affected toe in a tea bath. Steep two to three tea bags in warm water and let it cool down to a warm temperature. Soak your ingrown toenail for 15 to 20 minutes, two to three times a day. The tannic acid in the tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling, while its antibacterial properties prevent infection. Choose tea bags made from chamomile, black, or green tea for best results. Remember to trim and file your toenails straight across to prevent future ingrown toenails. Always consult a healthcare professional if your symptoms persist or worsen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ingrown toenail that are treated with herbal teas are athlete's foot and fungal nail infections. For athlete's foot, tea made from calendula or chamomile flowers can be used to soak the feet, as these herbs have anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties. For fungal nail infections, tea made from black tea or green tea can be applied directly to the affected nails, as the tannins in these teas can help to inhibit the growth of fungi. Additionally, tea made from oregano or thyme can be consumed as a tea or used as a foot soak, as these herbs have strong anti-fungal and anti-bacterial properties. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas to treat any medical condition.
